title: Tracking the next SDA challenge
summary: The U.S. government is facing challenges in monitoring the crowded and contested space environment due to rapid commercial innovation in sensors and AI-driven insights. The Space Development Agency (SDA) is tasked with addressing these monitoring challenges.

title: Preparing for the next NASA budget battle
summary: Congress passed a fiscal year 2026 appropriations bill in January, rejecting proposed steep cuts to NASA's budget, including a 25% overall reduction and nearly 50% cut to science. The space community welcomed the decision, avoiding significant funding reductions.

title: A lunar base or a lunar economy?
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman's vision for a lunar base lacks a long-term economic development plan, risking a missed opportunity for sustainable lunar economy growth.

title: LeoLabs debuts space monitoring tool for military users
summary: LeoLabs has introduced a new space monitoring tool called 'Delta' that goes beyond collision warnings to detect potential adversarial activities. This tool is specifically designed for military users.

title: Industry navigates NASA’s start-and-stop approach to commercial space stations
summary: NASA's plans to accelerate the replacement of the International Space Station have slowed down despite initial signals of acceleration. The industry is adapting to NASA's inconsistent approach to the Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) program.

title: Artemis 2 science gets underway as Orion begins its return trip
summary: Scientists are reviewing images and observations from the Artemis 2 mission as the Orion spacecraft begins its return to Earth. The mission marks a significant step in lunar exploration and scientific research.

title: Capella Space wins $49 million contract for military communications satellite demo
summary: Capella Space secured a $49 million contract from the Space Development Agency to demonstrate a military communications satellite by 2027. The award highlights Capella's role in advancing space-based communications for defense applications.

title: Space security moves to forefront as threats to satellites spread
summary: Space security has become a critical issue as threats to satellites increase, highlighted by a Secure World Foundation report. The report emphasizes the growing reliance on orbital systems and the spread of interference technologies.

title: Inspection cubesat demo planned as first step toward orbital defense mothership
summary: Bulgarian satellite manufacturer EnduroSat and British defense tech startup Shield Space plan to launch a maneuverable inspection cubesat next year. This demo is a precursor to developing a larger mothership satellite capable of deploying dozens of inspection cubesats for orbital defense missions.

title: 'Audible screams of delight' from NASA scientists over micrometeorite impacts on the moon witnessed by Artemis 2 astronauts
summary: NASA scientists are delighted by the Artemis 2 astronauts' observation of micrometeorite impacts on the moon during their flyby. The micrometeor impact flashes witnessed by the crew have generated excitement among the scientific community.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts spy the Milky Way | Space photo of the day for April 8, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a stunning space photo of the Milky Way's galactic plane. The image highlights the dense concentration of stars in our galaxy.

title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts capture space history — on their iPhones
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts are using iPhones to capture high-quality photos of the moon and Earth during their mission. This marks a historic moment in space exploration by integrating consumer technology.

title: Galaxy starves its supermassive black hole, loses 95% of its brightness
summary: A galaxy has experienced a dramatic 95% drop in brightness due to its supermassive black hole starving. This sudden dimming is a rare and significant event in astrophysical observations.

title: 'It just made it even more special': Being so far from Earth makes you appreciate our planet even more, Artemis 2 astronaut says
summary: Artemis 2 astronaut Christina Koch highlights the profound 'overview effect' of seeing Earth from beyond the moon, emphasizing how distance deepens appreciation for the planet. Koch reflects on how space travel transforms perspective, making Earth's beauty and fragility more apparent.

title: The Artemis 2 space toilet is actually working fine. But there is another problem
summary: The Artemis 2 space toilet is functioning properly, but engineers are addressing an issue with evacuating the tank.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' sound designers used surprising animal sounds to create Rocky's musical alien voice (interview)
summary: The sound designers for 'Project Hail Mary' used unique animal sounds to craft Rocky's alien voice, aiming to convey his 'sonic soul'.

title: Relive Artemis 2's epic moon flyby with these amazing photos
summary: NASA released stunning images from the Artemis 2 mission's historic moon flyby. The astronauts documented their journey around the moon in great detail.

title: A satellite just captured images of a tsunami being born. Here's why that's a big deal
summary: Scientists captured images of a tsunami forming using the SWOT satellite, providing unprecedented insights into tsunamigenesis. The 2025 Kamchatka tsunami observation marks a significant advancement in tsunami detection and study.
company: SWOT (Surface Water and Ocean Topography mission)

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Beams Official Moon Flyby Photos to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II astronauts captured the first flyby images of the Moon, revealing unseen regions and a rare in-space solar eclipse. The photos were taken during a seven-hour flyby of the lunar far side on April 6.

title: Earthset
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a view of Earth setting from the far side of the Moon on April 6, 2026, documenting lunar terrain features like impact craters and lava flows.

title: Experience the Rollout of SLS Hardware for Artemis III
summary: NASA invites digital creators and social media users to attend the rollout of the SLS core stage for Artemis III from Michoud Assembly Facility to Kennedy Space Center.

title: NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon
summary: NASA will host a media call with the Artemis II crew on April 8 at 9:45 p.m. EDT to discuss their historic mission around the Moon. The 20-minute virtual news conference will feature astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and a CSA representative.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission crew set a new record for the farthest human spaceflight. The Orion spacecraft's closest lunar approach was adjusted to about 4,067 miles.

title: NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview
summary: NASA's Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission will deliver around 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station using the Cygnus XL spacecraft. This is the second flight of the upgraded, more cargo-capable Cygnus XL version.

title: Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II
summary: The Night Sky Network celebrated the Artemis II mission's milestone on April 6, 2026, when the crew traveled farther than any humans in history by orbiting the far side of the Moon. The event can be rewatched on NASA’s YouTube channel or NASA+.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at its Starbase Pad 2 for the upcoming return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the company's South Texas launch site.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II set a record by conducting a successful lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This comes as NASA's Artemis II mission approaches its midpoint.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a key milestone for the mission carrying the first crewed lunar flyby since Apollo 17.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This event is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is involved in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the progress in space exploration and the role of private industry in supporting NASA's missions.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program, expanding access to space for multiple customers.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the provided news snippet without additional context or details.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication showcases milestones in European space exploration and innovation.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team used ESA's parabolic flight campaign to test graphene aerogels under microgravity, observing that lasers propelled the samples forward with controllable thrust. The findings, published in Advanced Science, suggest potential for light-based propulsion in space applications like solar sails.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed with versatile capabilities, potentially revolutionizing multiple applications in the space industry. The chip's multifunctional design could enhance efficiency and performance in satellite and spacecraft systems.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, focusing on industrial opportunities and program priorities. The events aim to facilitate direct dialogue between ESA and industry stakeholders.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Assembly and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, highlighting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. The post is from Payload, a space industry news outlet.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-developed radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. HERA will act as an early warning system for potential solar flare events.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: Advocates and lawmakers are preparing to oppose the Trump administration's proposed $18.8 billion NASA budget, which includes significant cuts to science and STEM missions. This marks a recurring conflict over NASA funding priorities.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish has secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with co-leads from Activate Capital and Shield Capital.

title: The State of EO 2026
summary: The Earth Observation (EO) industry is experiencing growth as more entities seek their own satellite capabilities. The article 'The State of EO 2026' highlights this trend and its benefits.

title: How Earthlings Got Views of the Lunar Fly By
summary: Redwire provided 11 cameras for the Orion capsule, enabling live views of its lunar flyby. These cameras were installed both inside and outside the spacecraft.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative ways to compete in an increasingly crowded industry.

title: Artemis II Supplier Series: Orion’s Windows
summary: The article discusses the significance of Orion's windows for astronauts on the Artemis II mission, highlighting the emotional and professional payoff of seeing space firsthand. It is part of a series covering suppliers for the Artemis II mission.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has requested an $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same funding level as the previous year's proposal. The budget includes cuts to NASA's science and STEM missions, similar to the fiscal 2026 request.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper' during an industry seminar. The post highlights the critical issue of securing insurance for these high-risk ventures.

title: It’s Unanimous: Space Already Functions as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Experts agree that space functions as critical infrastructure in the U.S., even though it is not officially designated as the 17th sector. The sector is deeply embedded in government, academia, and industry practices.

title: Cybersecurity in Space is Hard; In Cislunar Space, it’s Really Hard
summary: Cybersecurity in space is challenging due to the lack of centralized anomaly detection and regular patching capabilities. The difficulties are amplified in cislunar space, where traditional terrestrial cybersecurity strategies are less effective.

title: In-Space Mobility, Maturing From Last Miles to Superhighways
summary: NASA studied reusable 'space tug' systems in the late 1960s as part of the Space Transportation System (STS), marking an early effort in in-space mobility. The concept is now evolving into more advanced systems akin to 'superhighways' in space.

title: Is Europe Moving Fast Enough to Close its Space Defense Gaps?
summary: Europe is accelerating efforts to address its space defense gaps amid concerns about Russia's military buildup and potential threats to NATO within four years. The urgency stems from the need to enhance space-based capabilities to counter emerging security challenges.

title: Space Is the Best Offset Strategy
summary: The article discusses space as a key national security offset strategy, leveraging technological advantages to counter adversarial strengths. It emphasizes the strategic importance of space in modern defense.

title: The Seismic Shifts in the Satellite Communications Landscape are Far From Over
summary: The satellite communications market has undergone rapid changes in the past four years, with SpaceX's Starlink leading the deployment of thousands of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites. The industry continues to experience significant shifts.

title: Automation and Agility: How SSC Space Go Is Designed for the New Age of Ground
summary: SSC Space Go is designed to enhance automation and agility for accessing space, offering spacecraft operations, engineering, and ground network services. The company operates the Esrange launch center in Sweden as part of its mission to support customers in space access.

title: On-Orbit Services Startup Starfish Space Raises $110M to Execute Missions, Boost Production
summary: Starfish Space raised $110 million in a Series B funding round to execute orbital servicing missions and increase production. The funds will support contracted missions and boost manufacturing capabilities.

title: Canadian Navy to Trial All.Space Hydra 4 Terminal
summary: The Royal Canadian Navy will test the All.Space Hydra 4 multi-band, multi-orbit mobility terminal to inform short-term satcom architecture decisions. The Hydra 4 terminal is designed to enhance satellite communications capabilities.

title: Planet Details AI-Driven Object Detection Onboard Pelican-4 Satellite
summary: Planet Labs achieved a milestone by performing AI-driven object detection directly onboard its Pelican-4 satellite using an Nvidia Jetson Orin module. The company announced this technological advancement on Tuesday.
